articles.headline: Foxconn confirms ransomware attack disrupting North American factories
articles.summary: Taiwanese electronics manufacturing giant Foxconn says a cyberattack disrupted operations across multiple North American factories, with the Nitrogen ransomware gang claiming it exfiltrated eight terabytes of data. For businesses, this raises immediate supply-chain and continuity risks, especially for companies depending on electronics manufacturing capacity or just-in-time shipments.

articles.headline: What A.I. Did to My College Class
articles.summary: This opinion piece highlights how AI is changing student behavior, academic integrity, and expectations around knowledge work. For businesses, it underscores the accelerating normalization of AI-assisted writing and analysis, which will affect hiring, training, and how employers assess entry-level skills.

articles.headline: Top Tech News Today, May 15, 2026
articles.summary: Samsung’s 45,000-worker strike threat highlights how labor disruption can quickly affect electronics supply chains and production schedules. Business leaders should watch for downstream impacts on component availability, delivery timing, and pricing if the dispute escalates. The same news roundup also underscores broader AI and infrastructure pressures that can ripple through vendor planning.
